headline: Starbucks (SBUX) Q2 2026 Earnings Transcript
summary: Global comps were a strong 6% driven by terrific performance across the business, especially in the U.S., and our consolidated operating margin improved to 9.4%, up about 110 basis points.  This is Starbucks.  Green Apron Service is setting the standard for world-class customer service.

headline: Starbucks Gains After Upgrading Outlook as Turnaround Momentum Builds
summary: Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) lifted its full-year 2026 guidance on Tuesday, raising expectations for both comparable-store sales growth and adjusted earnings per share, as CEO Brian Niccol continues to push forward a turnaround strategy that is attracting more customers back to stores. The company now expects comparable store sales to grow by at least 5.

content.title: Starbucks Gains After Upgrading Outlook as Turnaround Momentum Builds
content.summary: Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) lifted its full-year 2026 guidance on Tuesday, raising expectations for both comparable-store sales growth and adjusted earnings per share, as CEO Brian Niccol continues to push forward a turnaround strategy that is attracting more customers back to stores. The company now expects comparable store sales to grow by at least 5.

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Seagate, Robinhood, Sandisk, Starbucks, and More
summary: FEATURE  Stock futures were rising on Wednesday ahead of the Federal Reserve’s policy decision and a slew of Big Tech earnings. These stocks were making moves in premarket trading:   Seagate Technology surged 17% after the hard-drive maker beat analysts’ earnings and sales targets for its fiscal third quarter and issued strong guidance, signaling to investors that data-storage demand remains robust.

headline: Canada Foodservice Market Report Forecast and Company Analysis Report 2026-2034: Starbucks, Domino's, Wendys, Seven & I, RBI, A&W, MTY, Performance, Papa John's, McDonald's, Berkshire Hathaway, Yum!
summary: The Canada Foodservice Market is forecast to expand from US$ 87.42 billion in 2025 to US$ 141.9 billion by 2034, driven by a 5.53% CAGR from 2026. Key factors include urbanization, rising dining service expenditures, and evolving consumer preferences. Growth is propelled by the surging popularity of Quick-Service Restaurants (QSRs), food delivery platforms, and digital ordering systems. The market faces challenges such as labor shortages and operating costs, yet opportunities in diverse, premium

headline: US stocks tick lower as oil prices keep spurting higher
summary: The U.S. stock market is ticking lower on Wednesday with the countdown to an afternoon announcement coming from the Federal Reserve on what it will do with interest rates.  Oil prices, meanwhile, continued to spurt higher because of the war with Iran.  The S&P 500 slipped 0.2%, a day after falling from its all-time high due to drops for artificial-intelligence stocks and worries about higher oil prices.

content.title: US stocks tick lower as oil prices keep spurting higher

headline: Starbucks employees call bonus targets 'impossible': CEO responds
summary: Starbucks (SBUX) reported stronger-than-expected quarterly results and raised its outlook. Starbucks CEO Brian Niccol sits down with Yahoo Finance to discuss the results, the turnaround success, and how the company is retaining the best talent.

headline: Wall Street Lifts Starbucks Price Targets as Comps Surge: Is the Turnaround Finally Real?
summary: Wall Street is rallying behind Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) after a Q2 FY2026 earnings report that the CEO calls the inflection point of the company’s turnaround. Four major firms raised their SBUX stock price targets following the April 28 release, with global comparable store sales rising 6% and North America (NA) comps climbing 7%. The takeaway for ... headline: US stocks drift in mixed trading as oil prices keep spurting higher
summary: The U.S. stock market is drifting in mixed trading Wednesday as the countdown ticks to an afternoon announcement from the Federal Reserve on what it will do with interest rates.  Oil prices, meanwhile, continued to spurt higher because of the war with Iran.  The S&P 500 slipped 0.1%, a day after falling from its all-time high due to drops for artificial-intelligence stocks and worries about higher oil prices.
